title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence · Execution Trace: Feed Go Migration Recovery
summary: Developers edit production tables manually, migrations mix irreversible data loss with schema changes, deploys assume zero old binaries, and rollback has never run. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go migration recovery increment in rehearsed schema and data migration chain. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Migration Recovery · Theory Feed Go Migration Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3020 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
